message:
security(node): adopt constant-time comparison for hash and MAC verification in 10 modules

Replaces direct == and != on security-sensitive hash/MAC strings
with ct_eq() from crate::security::constant_time, preventing
timing side-channel attacks:

- ecosystem_compliance.rs: tamper-evidence hash verification
- provenance.rs: signed_payload_hash chain validation (+new test)
- proof_carrying_decode.rs: payload + signature hash verification
  (+new test for invalid payload hash)
- rollback_bundle.rs: checksum + integrity hash verification
  (+new tamper test for length mismatch)
- state_model.rs: import ct_eq for state hash comparison
- universal_verifier_sdk.rs: capsule signature verification
- revocation_freshness_gate.rs: signature proof verification
- verifier_economy/mod.rs: duplicate submission detection on
  execution trace hash
- claim_compiler.rs: contract signature verification (+refined
  tamper test using character replacement)
- evidence_replay_gate.rs: input hash verification (+new test
  for truncated input hash detection)

All comparisons now use constant-time byte-level XOR accumulation,
eliminating information leakage through comparison timing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
